meaning: n.土阿莫土语(南太平洋土阿莫土群岛上通用的波利尼西亚语)
examples: A school of fish clusters near a reef in French Polynesia's Tuamotu Archipelago.
This astronaut photograph features Mataiva atoll, the westernmost atoll of the Tuamotu chain.
The Tuamotu Archipelago is part of French Polynesia, and forms the largest chain of atolls in the world.
To the east lie the Tuamotu Islands, 78 low coral atolls, where locals rely mainly on copra (dried coconut) and black pearls for income.
